metadata.source: EU ETS Benchmark Decision (EU) 2021/927 (Annex I), Commission Delegated Regulation (EU) 2019/331, Regulation (EU) 2023/956 Article 31
metadata.description: EU ETS product benchmarks for free allocation mapped to CBAM products. The CBAM obligation is reduced by the amount of free allowances that would have been allocated to an equivalent EU installation producing the same goods.
metadata.disclaimer: Benchmark values are from Phase 4 (2021-2025) and may be updated for 2026-2030. The phase-out schedule follows Regulation 2023/956 Article 31. For official values, consult the EU Commission implementing acts.
metadata.benchmark_basis: Average of the 10% most efficient installations in the EU (2016-2017 data for 2021-2025 period)

phase_out_schedule.description: Progressive phase-out of free allocation for CBAM sectors, mirrored by phase-in of CBAM certificates. Article 31 of Regulation (EU) 2023/956.

2025.notes: Last year of full free allocation for CBAM sectors. Transitional CBAM reporting only.

2026.notes: First year of definitive CBAM. 2.5% of CBAM certificates must be surrendered (net of free allocation reduction).

2035.notes: Full CBAM application. Zero free allocation for CBAM-covered sectors. EU producers purchase 100% of allowances.

cement_clinker.source: EU ETS Benchmark Decision (EU) 2021/927, Annex I, Row 1
cement_clinker.activity_level_definition: Tonnes of clinker produced (dry process equivalent)
cement_clinker.system_boundary: From raw material preparation to clinker cooler outlet, including on-site fossil fuel combustion
cement_clinker.notes: Most relevant CBAM benchmark. Covers ~60% of cement sector emissions. Updated every 5 years based on average of 10% most efficient.

grey_cement.source: Derived from clinker benchmark x average clinker ratio (0.72)
grey_cement.system_boundary: From clinker production through grinding and blending
grey_cement.notes: Effective benchmark depends on clinker-to-cement ratio. Lower ratio = lower emissions per tonne cement.

white_cement.source: EU ETS Benchmark Decision (EU) 2021/927, Annex I
white_cement.activity_level_definition: Tonnes of white cement clinker produced
white_cement.system_boundary: From raw material preparation to clinker cooler outlet
white_cement.notes: Higher benchmark than grey clinker due to purity requirements (iron-free raw mix, higher kiln temperature).

hot_metal.source: EU ETS Benchmark Decision (EU) 2021/927, Annex I, Row 3
hot_metal.activity_level_definition: Tonnes of liquid hot metal produced at the blast furnace taphole
hot_metal.system_boundary: From coke oven to blast furnace taphole, including sinter plant
hot_metal.notes: Key benchmark for integrated steelworks. Covers BF operations including sinter and coke.

crude_steel_bof.product_name: Crude steel (BOF route - basic oxygen furnace)
crude_steel_bof.source: Derived from hot metal benchmark + BOF steelmaking addition
crude_steel_bof.activity_level_definition: Tonnes of crude steel produced via BOF route
crude_steel_bof.system_boundary: From raw materials to crude steel (including BF, BOF, continuous casting)
crude_steel_bof.notes: Full integrated route. Most carbon-intensive steelmaking pathway.

crude_steel_eaf.product_name: Crude steel (EAF route - electric arc furnace)
crude_steel_eaf.source: EU ETS Benchmark Decision (EU) 2021/927, Annex I
crude_steel_eaf.activity_level_definition: Tonnes of crude steel produced via EAF (carbon steel)
crude_steel_eaf.system_boundary: From scrap/DRI charging to crude steel at continuous caster
crude_steel_eaf.notes: Direct emissions only. Indirect electricity emissions handled separately. 100% scrap basis.

sintered_ore.source: EU ETS Benchmark Decision (EU) 2021/927, Annex I
sintered_ore.system_boundary: From raw material blending to sinter cooler
sintered_ore.notes: Sub-benchmark within integrated steelworks. Covered under hot metal benchmark for integrated sites.

iron_casting.source: EU ETS Benchmark Decision (EU) 2021/927, Annex I
iron_casting.system_boundary: From melting to finished casting (including moulding, cooling, fettling)
iron_casting.notes: Covers cupola and electric induction furnace routes. Direct emissions benchmark.

unwrought_aluminium.source: EU ETS Benchmark Decision (EU) 2021/927, Annex I
unwrought_aluminium.activity_level_definition: Tonnes of unwrought primary aluminium (liquid metal at potline)
unwrought_aluminium.system_boundary: From alumina to liquid aluminium at potline, including anode production
unwrought_aluminium.notes: Direct emissions only (anode effect PFCs + carbon anode consumption). Indirect electricity emissions (~14.5 MWh/t) are separately calculated and critically important for total footprint.

aluminium_pre_bake_anode.source: EU ETS Benchmark Decision (EU) 2021/927, Annex I
aluminium_pre_bake_anode.system_boundary: From green anode paste to baked anode
aluminium_pre_bake_anode.notes: Applies to standalone anode plants. Integrated smelters include anode emissions in the smelter benchmark.

aluminium_soderberg_paste.source: EU ETS Benchmark Decision (EU) 2021/927, Annex I
aluminium_soderberg_paste.system_boundary: From raw materials to electrode paste
aluminium_soderberg_paste.notes: Soderberg technology being phased out globally. Higher process emissions at smelter level than pre-bake.

ammonia.source: EU ETS Benchmark Decision (EU) 2021/927, Annex I
ammonia.activity_level_definition: Tonnes of ammonia produced (100% NH3 basis)
ammonia.system_boundary: From natural gas feed to ammonia product, including syngas generation, shift, CO2 removal, and synthesis
ammonia.notes: SMR route. ~60% feedstock CO2 (process) + ~40% fuel CO2. If CO2 is captured for urea, net emissions are lower but benchmark applies to gross production.

nitric_acid.source: EU ETS Benchmark Decision (EU) 2021/927, Annex I
nitric_acid.system_boundary: From ammonia oxidation to nitric acid concentration, including N2O abatement
nitric_acid.notes: N2O from Pt-Rh catalytic oxidation. Best available technology (BAT) abatement achieves >95% N2O reduction. Benchmark reflects modern abatement.

urea.source: EU ETS Benchmark Decision (EU) 2021/927, Annex I
urea.activity_level_definition: Tonnes of urea produced (prilled or granular)
urea.system_boundary: From ammonia and CO2 to urea product
urea.notes: Net of CO2 chemically bound in urea (0.733 tCO2/t urea). Gross ammonia emissions minus sequestered CO2.

mixed_fertilisers.source: Derived from component benchmarks weighted by typical formulation
mixed_fertilisers.system_boundary: From precursor chemicals to blended/compound fertiliser product
mixed_fertilisers.notes: Highly variable depending on N:P:K ratio and precursors used. For specific products, calculate from constituent benchmarks.

hydrogen_smr.source: EU ETS Benchmark Decision (EU) 2021/927, Annex I
hydrogen_smr.activity_level_definition: Tonnes of hydrogen produced (100% H2 basis)
hydrogen_smr.system_boundary: From natural gas to purified hydrogen, including reformer, WGS, PSA
hydrogen_smr.notes: Conventional SMR grey hydrogen. ~9-11 tCO2/tH2 at world average. EU best practice ~8.85 tCO2/tH2.

hydrogen_electrolysis.source: EU ETS Benchmark Decision - Direct emissions are zero for electrolysis
hydrogen_electrolysis.activity_level_definition: Tonnes of hydrogen produced via water electrolysis
hydrogen_electrolysis.system_boundary: From water and electricity to purified hydrogen
hydrogen_electrolysis.notes: Zero direct emissions. Indirect emissions from electricity are critical (50-55 MWh/tH2). Green hydrogen (certified renewable electricity) qualifies for zero total embedded emissions under certain conditions.

electricity.source: EU ETS Benchmark Decision - Electricity generation sub-installation
electricity.system_boundary: From fuel input to net electricity output at generator terminals
electricity.notes: Applies to direct interconnection electricity imports. For embedded indirect emissions in other CBAM goods, installation-specific or country grid factors are preferred.

cross_sectoral_correction_factor.description: Cross-sectoral correction factor (CSCF) applied to free allocation to ensure total free allocation does not exceed the EU ETS cap for the industry sector.
cross_sectoral_correction_factor.notes: CSCF was not triggered in Phase 4 (2021-2025) as preliminary allocation fit within the industry cap. Projected values for 2026-2030 reflect expected cap tightening. Actual values depend on annual allocation vs cap calculations. Applied multiplicatively with the CBAM phase-out factor.

benchmark_update_methodology.description: How product benchmarks are recalculated for each allocation period

calculation_method.step_1: Collect verified emission data from all EU ETS installations producing the product
calculation_method.step_2: Rank installations by emission intensity (tCO2e per tonne of product)
calculation_method.step_3: Calculate the average of the 10% most efficient installations (lowest emitters)
calculation_method.step_4: This average becomes the benchmark value
calculation_method.step_5: Apply annual reduction rate to derive benchmark for each year within the period

annual_reduction_rates.determination: Based on the gap between the benchmark and the median installation performance. Larger gaps lead to faster reduction (up to 1.6%/yr). The standard rate of 0.63% approximates technological improvement pace.

carbon_leakage_list.description: Sectors on the carbon leakage list receive higher free allocation (up to 100% of benchmark before CBAM phase-out applies).
carbon_leakage_list.notes: All CBAM sectors are on the carbon leakage list. From 2026, free allocation for these sectors is progressively reduced per the CBAM phase-out schedule above.

cbam_certificate_calculation_example.description: Illustrative example of CBAM certificate calculation for an import in 2028

calculation_steps.step_4_notes: Net cost is the number of CBAM certificates to surrender times the weekly average EU ETS price.
